[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2017-06-07
[TITLE]: Brazilian gun seizures panorama | thefirearmblog.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: Brazilian gun seizures panorama We have here at TFB given occasional news of different guns being seized by local authorities in Brazil. Weapons of all kinds have fallen into the hands of police agencies on a nationwide basis, but the...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: To complement the news, here’s a small selection of photos of some recent gun seizure occurrences in Brazil, showing not the only quantities, but the variety of items involved. 
 This article had just been uploaded for publication when my cell phone buzzed and I received the three photos reproduced below, showing this uncommon (in this neck of the woods, at least) gun seized by 41 BPM (41st Military Police Battalion) in Rio’s Acari community: a bolt-action 5.56x45mm/.223 Remington rifle (see 30-round magazine) fitted with a Stoeger (shotgun and airgun manufacturer) stock, a cheap Comet 3-9x40EG scope (for airgun/airsoft rifles), and a folding bipod. What can you, TFB readers, say about it? Or should yours truly be immediately able to ID it? I just couldn’t!

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2010
[TITLE]: Fact check: Gun bust at border not a U.S. job at all
[METADATA/INTRO]: Fact check: Gun bust at border not a U.S. job at all Mexican Army seized piles of weapons nowhere near Arizona....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The report said the collection included more than 130 assault rifles, as well as machine guns, a rocket launcher, hand grenades and grenade launchers, ammunition, SUVs and military clothing. 
 Contrary to what the chain e-mail says, there were some reports of the raid in U.S. media outlets. 
 The Houston Chronicle and Newsweek magazine reported that the camp was run by the Zetas drug cartel. The Chronicle said that particular raid was the third in about five weeks as the Mexican government was trying to stiffen its resolve to eliminate gang activities.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Mexican Drug Cartel Weapon Raid
[METADATA/INTRO]: Mexican Drug Cartel Weapon Raid Guns Found...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Mexican Drug Cartel Weapon Raid 
 It's estimated that they found around 200 rifles, and 30 pistols along with grenade and rocket launchers.Investigation 
 At least 25 suspects managed to get away.Other Explosives Found 
 Along with these weapons, the Mexican Army found 12 trucks/SUVs under a shaded canopy.The vehicles contained military & police issue accessories.
